title: quercus graciliformis f. parvilobata
intro: Quercus graciliformis f. parvilobata, also known as the Chinese cork oak, is a variant of the Quercus graciliformis species, a type of evergreen tree native to China. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Quercus graciliformis f. parvilobata include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as the prevention of cardiovascular diseases and cancer. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being dried and used in capsule or powder form for medicinal purposes.
